title: Ambulance Drivers and Attendants, Except Emergency Medical Technicians
description: Drive ambulance or assist ambulance driver in transporting sick, injured, or convalescent persons. Assist in lifting patients.

tasks.text: Remove and replace soiled linens or equipment to maintain sanitary conditions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drive ambulances or assist ambulance drivers in transporting sick, injured, or convalescent persons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Report facts concerning accidents or emergencies to hospital personnel or law enforcement officials.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place patients on stretchers, and load stretchers into ambulances, usually with assistance from other attendants.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Accompany and assist emergency medical technicians on calls.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Replace supplies and disposable items on ambulances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform minor maintenance on emergency medical services vehicles, such as ambulances.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Clean and wash rigs, ambulances, or equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Administer first aid, such as bandaging, splinting, or administering oxygen.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
